summary: This is an extremely brief post celebrating being in Boston, presumably during the Red Sox's 2013 World Series victory when David Ortiz (Big Papi) was the MVP. The post consists of a single sentence expressing excitement about the timing of being in the city. It captures a moment of local sports enthusiasm rather than O'Neil's typical analytical content.
key_points: The post celebrates being in Boston during a significant moment (likely the 2013 World Series win)
David Ortiz ('Big Papi') is referenced affectionately in the title
This represents a rare personal/lighthearted post rather than O'Neil's usual analytical work
money_quotes: Kind of awesome to be in Boston today.
key_insight: Even data scientists take breaks from algorithm critique to celebrate hometown sports victories.
